title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man with a sword who allegedly attacked officers near Northeastern University.
The suspect, allegedly experiencing a mental health crisis, was shot after stabbing a police officer and injuring an EMS clinician.
Several recent violent incidents have occurred in the area, with students and others injured in separate stabbings.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois from Hudson, NH is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her late godmother who died of brain cancer.
Dubois qualified for the marathon at the Lake Erie Marathon in September 2025 and is running to support MGH's Cancer Care Equity program.
The Cancer Care Equity program at Massachusetts General Hospital assists families with incidental costs during cancer treatments.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A woman in her 40s died in a house fire at 80 Glendale Ave., Brockton, early Saturday morning.
Two other individuals were hospitalized following the fire, which engulfed the second floor of the home.
Firefighters and investigators are examining the cause, which is not currently seen as suspicious.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, shot and wounded a Nottingham officer while police attempted to arrest him in Raymond, NH.
Police found Masse in the woods after he fired at family, prompting a shelter-in-place advisory.
Masse was killed in a shootout with New Hampshire State Police; an autopsy is pending.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon from Canton, Mass., will run the Boston Marathon after surviving breast cancer and multiple surgeries.
Gannon's best friend, Peggy, who also survived cancer, will join her in running the marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Both women aim to inspire others battling cancer by completing the marathon, highlighting perseverance and survivorship.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' on April 4, marking his fifth time as host, joining the show's five-timers club.
Jack White served as the musical guest, also making his fifth solo appearance on 'Saturday Night Live'.
The episode featured a cameo-heavy five-timers sketch and a music-based sketch with Black performing 'Seven Nation Army'.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledges past struggles with his left hand, attributing them to injuries and a major wrist surgery.
Since recovering from injuries, Brown's performance has improved, averaging career-high points, rebounds, and assists this season.
Brown's enhanced skills have placed him in MVP talks and helped secure a top-4 playoff seed for the Celtics.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returns to Boston as Padres announcer, 11 years after leaving NESN as Red Sox play-by-play voice.
Red Sox fans warmly greeted Orsillo, who expressed gratitude for his memorable tenure and ongoing fan support.
Orsillo reconnected with NESN colleagues Jim Rice and Tom Caron, sharing moments on social media during his visit.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Alex Cora slots Roman Anthony as DH due to needing defensive improvements in the outfield.
Anthony contributed offensively with a triple and a base hit against the Padres, despite defensive struggles.
Red Sox hold a 2-6 record as they enter Sunday's game against Padres at Fenway Park.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two runs on three hits and four walks over four innings against Padres.
Aroldis Chapman gave up a ninth-inning run leading to Red Sox's 3-2 loss to San Diego Padres.
Early's 39 strikeouts in six career games tie Red Sox record with Daisuke Matsuzaka for most strikeouts in initial starts.
